mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically elevating equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates sharply, compressing corporate revenue growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or forward returns are negative when both series expand concurrently over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum in equity markets as investors price in immediate earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, yielding negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with a constrained policy rate compresses real yields, prompting equity deleveraging and generating negative forward returns as discount rates adjust upward.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.3% monthly while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%, invalidating the real-rate compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and consumption, supporting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or positive payroll revisions coincide with negative equity performance in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth falls below 0.5 percent, corporate earnings forecasts are systematically downgraded, triggering risk-off positioning and negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth is below 0.5%, or the threshold loses statistical significance after adjusting for lagged market momentum.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent improvement in industrial output and declining unemployment confirms a synchronized labor-supply recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields negative predictive power, or 60-day returns are negative when both conditions are met in more than 60% of historical observations.
